title: Friday Sermon 15 March 1985
description:
A Review of the Pakistani Government's 'White Paper' Qadiyaniyyat— A Grave Threat to Islam: Glorious Services of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community during the Tragedy of the Palestinian Muslims.

In 1984 the Islamic government of Pakistan set aside all Islamic injunctions and took upon itself the burden of depriving the Ahmadi Muslims of many basic human rights including religious and social freedoms. In an attempt to justify this action, the government of Pakistan published a so-called White Paper under the title 'Qadiyaniyyat - Islam Key Liyey Sangin Khatrah' ['Qadiyaniyyat - A Grave Threat to Islam'].

Although there was nothing new in this so-called White Paper and the Community literature already included detailed answers to all the issues which were raised, nevertheless Hazrat Mirza Tahir Ahmad - Khalifatul Masih IV, the then Imam of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community, answered these allegations in a series of Friday Sermons.

This sermon deals with the allegation that the Ahmadis are (God forbid) agents of Israel. The Caliph points out that, as commanded by 'The Holy Quran' and exemplified by the Holy Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, Ahmadis have been actively spreading the message of Islam all over the world, including in Israel. It also gives detail about the glorious services rendered by the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community during the tragedy of the Palestinian Muslims.

The text http://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST19850315-UR.pdf
English translation http://www.alislam.org/library/books/AhmadiyyahMuslim-and-PalestinianMuslims.pdf
